draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high-contrast, dark backgrounds to reduce cognitive load and support long working sessions
facade.label: Dark interface
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker color schemes, high contrast text, night-mode aesthetics, reduced brightness for focus
facade.rejectImplies: Explore lighter backgrounds, softer contrast, bright/airy interfaces, daylight-friendly design

evidence.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer high-contrast, dark backgrounds to reduce cognitive load and support long working sessions
evidence.implication: Explore darker color schemes, high contrast text, night-mode aesthetics, reduced brightness for focus

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, approachable tone over formal corporate language in their AI workspace
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: Build towards warm, human-centered voice; use contractions and everyday language; invite personality in copy and interactions
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward polished, professional tone; maintain formal distance; use precise, corporate language; minimize personality markers

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer rounded, gentle edges over sharp geometry in AI interfaces
facade.label: Soft corners
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should feel approachable and less clinical—rounded buttons, curved panels, organic spacing
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace needs crisp, defined edges and geometric precision—sharp corners, clean lines, structured grid aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer glanceable real-time data over narrative summaries
facade.label: Live metrics dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should surface live stats, progress indicators, and numeric KPIs prominently in primary views
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace should favor narrative descriptions, status prose, and contextual explanations over numeric displays

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate feedback (no transition delay) for AI workspace interactions
facade.axisTargeted: Latency vs. fluidity — snappy instant vs. smooth animated
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and directness; may tolerate abrupt state changes if feedback is immediate
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers motion and continuity; willing to wait milliseconds for smooth transitions that feel coherent

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent left navigation over top tabs for AI workspace task switching
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure: persistent sidebar vs collapsible vs tab-based
facade.label: Sidebar always visible
facade.acceptImplies: Persistent navigation is valued; likely indicates preference for quick task switching and constant context awareness. May imply workspace with multiple simultaneous threads or projects.
facade.rejectImplies: User favors cleaner, minimal header or may want more screen real estate. Could indicate preference for modal/dialog task switching or simpler single-focus workflows.

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer rounded, gentle interface elements over sharp angles to feel approachable and reduce visual tension during intensive work sessions
facade.label: Soft corners
facade.acceptImplies: Explore rounded buttons, curved edges on cards, smooth radius on containers, friendly pill-shaped inputs, organic shapes in UI components
facade.rejectImplies: Consider sharp, geometric, or angular design language; crisp edges on elements; rectangular forms with minimal border-radius; more structured, technical appearance

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
